solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public List<String> findRepeatedDnaSequences(String s) {
        Set<String> seen = new HashSet<>();
        Set<String> repeated = new HashSet<>();

        for (int i = 0; i <= s.length() - 10; i++) {
            String sequence = s.substring(i, i + 10);
            if (!seen.add(sequence)) {
                repeated.add(sequence);
            }
        }

        return new ArrayList<>(repeated);
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses two hash sets to track seen and repeated sequences. It iterates through the DNA string, extracting each 10-letter substring and checking for duplicates. The time complexity is linear with respect to the input size, and the space complexity is also linear due to the hash sets. The main strength is its simplicity and clarity, but it may use more memory for large inputs.>
